title: Get audience details
content:
Use this endpoint to obtain the details of specified audiences, such as current status and modification record. Details for both owned and shared audiences will be returned. You can use `is_creator` to check whether this advertiser is the owner of this audience. > **Note**
A maximum of 100 audiences can be queried per request. ## Comparing v1.2 and v1.3 The following table outlines the differences between v1.2 and v1.3 endpoints. ```xtable |Changes{30%}|v1.2{35%}|v1.3{35%}| |---|---|---| |Endpoint path|/v1.2/dmp/custom_audience/get/|/v1.3/dmp/custom_audience/get/| |Request parameter data type|`advertiser_id`: number
`custom_audience_ids`: number[]|`advertiser_id`: string
`custom_audience_ids`: string[]| |New request parameter |/|`history_size`: number| |New response parameter|/|`shared`: boolean
`is_creator`: boolean
`create_time`: string: string
`owner_id`: string
`lookalike_spec`: object[]
` source_audience_id`: string
`include_source`: boolean
`mobile_os`: string
`placements`: string
`location_ids`: string[]
`audience_size`: string |Response parameter data type|`audience_id`: number|`audience_id`: string| ``` ## Request **Endpoint** https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/dmp/custom_audience/get/ **Method** GET **Header** ``` xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738373164380162).| ``` **Parameters** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |advertiser_id {Required}|string| Advertiser ID. | |custom_audience_ids {Required}|string[]|A list of Audience IDs. Length range is ` [1, 100] `.| |history_size|number|The size of the history data which the user requests.| ``` ### Example ``` xcodeblock (code curl http) curl --get -H "Access-Token:xxx" \\ --data-urlencode "advertiser_id=ADVERTISER_ID" \\ --data-urlencode "custom_audience_ids=[\"CUSTOM_AUDIENCE_IDS\"]" \\ https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/dmp/custom_audience/get/ (/code) ``` ## Response ``` xtable |Field{35%}|Data Type{15%}|Description{50%}| |---|---|---| |code |number|Response code. For the complete list of response codes and descriptions, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |message |string|Response message. For details, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |data |object|Returned Data.| #|list |object[]|A list of audience details.| ##|audience_details |object|Audience data.| ###|audience_id |string|Audience ID.| ###|msg|string|Information about audience status.| ###|audience_sub_type|string| Audience sub type, indicating the type of ads that the audience can be used. Enum values: `NORMAL`: Normal audience. It can be used in non-R&F ads. `REACH_FREQUENCY`: Audience for Reach & Frequency. It can only be used in R&F ads.| ###|error_msg |string|Error information when creating or modifying an audience. | ###|is_valid |boolean|Audience availability. An audience becomes available after it has been processed. An audience becomes invalid after it is expired. | ###|is_expiring|boolean|Whether an audience is expiring. When an audience is expiring, it will expire in less than 60 days. | ###|expired_time|string|Time when the audience expires, in the format of "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS".| ###|name |string|Audience name.| ###|rule |string|If this audience is an engagement audience, app activity audience or website traffic audience, this is the creation rule for it.| ###|is_auto_refresh |boolean|If this audience is an engagement audience / app activity audience or website traffic audience, this indicates whether the audience refreshes daily.| ###|shared |boolean|Whether this audience is shared with others. Enum values: `True`, `False`. ###|is_creator |boolean|Whether this advertiser is the owner of the audience. Enum values: `true`, `false`. You can use `shared` and `is_creator` to check audience ownership and shared status. The rules are as follows:
1. `shared` = `true` and `is_creator` = `true`: The advertiser is the owner and the audience is shared to others.
2. `shared` = `true` and `is_creator` = `false`: The advertiser is NOT the owner and the audience is shared by others.
3. `shared` = `false` and `is_creator` = `true`: The advertiser is the owner and the audience has not been shared to others. ###|owner_id| string | Advertiser ID of the audience creator.
  • If `is_creator`=`true`, `owner_id` will be `advertiser_id` that you specified in the request.
  • If `is_creator`=`false`, `owner_id` will be the ID of the advertiser that created the audience.
| ###|create_time|string|Time when the audience was created, in the format of "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS". ###|type |string|Audience type. Enum values:
  • `Customer File Audience` ([Customer File Audience](https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article?aid=6721963343558475781)).
  • `Engagement Audience` ([Engagement Audience](https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article?aid=6721963298155134982)).
  • `App Activity Audience` ([App Activity Audience](https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article?aid=6721962999092871173)).
  • `Website Audience` ([Website Traffic Audience](https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article?aid=6721962903081058309)).
  • `Lookalike Audience` ([Lookalike Audience](https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article?aid=6746381707865227270)).
  • `Lead Generation `([Lead Generation Audience](https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article/lead-generation-audience?lang=en))
  • `Rule Business Account` ([Business Account Audience](https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article?aid=10011405&redirected=1))
  • `Rule ad shop` ([Shop Activity Audience](https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article/shop-retargeting?lang=en))
  • `Partner` (Partner Audience)
  • `Challenge` (Challenge Audience)
  • `Preferred Population` (Premium Audience)

Note: Currently, Partner Audience, Challenge Audience and Premium Audience cannot be created through the API.| ###|cover_num |number|TikTok coverage, or number of matched users on TikTok.| ###|calculate_type {+Conditional}|string |Encryption type. For enum values, see [Enumeration - Encryption Type](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737174886619138).| ###|lookalike_spec |object[]|Lookalike audience-specific information.
Returned only when the audience is a lookalike audience.| ####|source_audience_id|string|Source (seed) audience ID. | ####|include_source|boolean|Whether to include the source audience in the new lookalike audience.| ####|mobile_os|string|Device operating systems. Enum values support `ALL` for both `ANDROID` and `IOS`. See [Enumeration-Audience Management-Lookalike System](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737174886619138) for details.| ####|placements|string|The apps where you want to deliver your ads. Enum values: `TikTok`, `TopBuzz & BuzzVideo`, `Pangle`. See [Enumeration - Audience Management-Lookalike Placement](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737174886619138) for details.| ####|location_ids|string[]|IDs of the locations that you want to target. For enum values, see [Enumeration-Lookalike Locations](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737174886619138).| ####|audience_size|string|Size of lookalike audience. Enum values: `NARROW`, `BALANCED` and `BROAD`.| ##|audience_history |object[]|List of audience modification history. | ###|action |string|Modification made. To learn about the meaning of the values, see "Mapping between `action` value and operation via /dmp/custom_audience/update/" section below.| ###|action_detail |string|Modification result. | ###|editor |string|Operator used. If modification was made through the TikTok API for Business, the value is "open_api".| ###|msg |string|If the operation is partial success, the information will be shown
here.| ###|opt_time |string|The operation time. Formats such as：2019-04-14 06:52:56.| |request_id |string|The log id of a request, which uniquely identifies the request.| ``` ### Example ``` xcodeblock (code Success-Response http) HTTPS/1.1 200 OK { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "123", "data": { "list": [ { "audience_history": [ { "msg": null, "action_detail": "6489086", "editor": "open_api", "opt_time": "2022-05-26 14:12:12", "action": "create" } ], "audience_details": { "audience_id": "123", "is_expiring": false, "is_auto_refresh": false, "is_creator": true, "expired_time": "2023-09-07 18:34:20", "is_valid": true, "shared": true, "create_time": "2022-05-26 14:12:12", "msg": null, "error_msg": null, "type": "Lookalike Audience", "audience_sub_type": "NORMAL", "calculate_type": null, "cover_num": 6489086, "name": "CUSTOM_AUDIENCE_NAME", "rule": "{\"inclusions\": null, \"exclusions\": null}", "owner_id": "{{owner_id}}", "lookalike_spec": null } } ] } } (/code); ``` ### Mapping between `action` value and operation via /dmp/custom_audience/update/ ``` xtable | action value{20%} | Description{35%} | Corresponding operation via /dmp/custom_audience/update/{45%} | |---|---|---| | `modify` | The name of the Custom Audience has been edited. | `custom_audience_name` is passed | | `upload - replace` | Files have been uploaded to replace the Customer File audience. | `file_paths` is passed and `action` is set as `REPLACE` or not passed | | `upload - add` | Files have been uploaded to expand the Customer File audience. | `file_paths` is passed and `action` is set as `APPEND` | | `upload remove` | Files have been uploaded to remove the Customer File audience. | `file_paths` is passed and `action` is set as `REMOVE` | | `convert_to_rf` | The Custom Audience has been enabled to be used in Reach & Frequency campaigns. | `audience_sub_type` is passed and set as `REACH_FREQUENCY` | ```
